hypothesis: SL=0.5% caps position sizing at ₹50k notional (only ₹250 risk deployed vs intended ₹500). Wider SL lets full ₹500 risk deploy → better-trained model + higher net PnL per trade.
verdict: REJECTED — wider SL consistently destroys the strategy
method: Walk-forward (expanding window, 4 folds, fixed thr 0.80) on the SAME 500-symbol universe for each SL config. PnL labels in the dataset encode the SL/TP; the model only selects bars. Narrow-SL control isolates the SL effect from the universe expansion (152→500 symbols, Phase 35).
mechanism: Tight 0.5% stop creates a high-bar label (only genuinely strong setups survive net-positive). Wider SL dilutes the label → model at thr 0.80 admits many low-quality trades (₹34/tr at 1.5%, −₹77/tr at 2.0%). Per-trade quality collapses monotonically as SL widens.
conclusion: Keep SL=0.5%/TP=5.0% deployment. The position-sizing cap at ₹50k is NOT a defect to fix via wider SL — it is what makes the label informative. Original deployment model (trained on 152 symbols) is still valid and unchanged.
bonus_finding: The narrow-SL control on the expanded 500-symbol universe gives +₹223,727 OOS (vs old +₹49,985 on 152 symbols). Retraining the deployment model on the 500-symbol narrow dataset would likely improve the live sleeve — separate recommendation, not part of this test.

files.source_edits: scripts/train_ml_strategy.py: meta sl_pct/tp_pct now read from dataset module; added --out-model/--out-meta args (non-destructive, deployment model unchanged)
